title: Carrier-doping effect and anomalous transport properties in Ni-doped CeCoIn5 investigated by Hall resistivity measurements
authors: Ryosuke Koizumi, Hayao Fujimoto, Teppei Takahashi, Azumi Yashiro, Haruna Kawakami, Kazuki Ishii, Hinako Kosaka, Takeshi Hasegawa, Yusei Shimizu, Ai Nakamura, Dai Aoki, Kenichi Tenya, Makoto Yokoyama
abstract: We investigated the effects of Ni doping on carrier density and anomalous electrical transport properties in CeCo$_{1-x}$Ni$_x$In$_5$ ($x \leq 0.3$) by performing Hall resistivity measurements. The carrier density, estimated from the Hall coefficient $R_{\rm H}$ at a temperature of 0.5 K in high magnetic fields, increases linearly with $x$, indicating that the doped Ni ions act as electron dopants. In CeCoIn$_5$, the magnitude of $-R_{\rm H}$ is strongly enhanced at magnetic fields near the superconducting upper critical field $H_{c2}$ and in the low-field region above the superconducting transition temperature $T_c$. However, these anomalies are found to be significantly suppressed by Ni doping. Possible origins of this suppression in $-R_{\rm H}$ are discussed.
summary: This paper demonstrates that Ni-doping in the heavy-fermion superconductor CeCoIn5 increases the electron carrier density and suppresses anomalous transport properties. The findings suggest that Ni modifies the electronic band structure rather than acting as a simple impurity, and that the suppression of Hall anomalies is decoupled from the quantum critical point at the upper critical field.
main_problem: The study investigates how Ni-doping affects carrier density and the anomalous Hall resistivity features in the heavy-fermion superconductor CeCoIn5.
main_result: Ni-doping acts as an electron dopant that increases carrier density and suppresses the anomalous enhancement of the Hall coefficient near the upper critical field and in the low-field region.
method: Hall resistivity and electrical resistivity measurements were performed on CeCo1-xNixIn5 single crystals using a PPMS across various temperatures and magnetic fields.
model_or_system: The physical system is the heavy-fermion superconductor CeCo1-xNixIn5 (x <= 0.3), a compensated multi-band metal with a tetragonal structure exhibiting superconducting, non-Fermi liquid, and Fermi liquid phases.
key_observables: Hall coefficient (RH), Hall resistivity, carrier density (n), electrical resistivity (rho), and temperature derivatives of resistivity.
important_parameters: Ni concentration (x), temperature (T), magnetic field (H), and the crossover temperatures between NFL and FL states.
main_assumptions: The estimation of carrier density assumes the high-field, low-temperature limit (0.5 K, 9 T) isolates the normal electronic contribution from anomalous terms.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows Ni concentration agreement and the H-T-x phase diagram; Fig 2 displays the temperature dependence of RH and phase diagrams; Fig 3 shows RH field dependence and carrier density vs. Ni concentration; Fig 4 shows the suppression of RH anomalies with increasing x.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the Ni-doped CeCoIn5 system, details the experimental methods and sample characterization, presents the temperature and field dependence of Hall and resistivity data, analyzes the evolution of carrier density, and discusses the physical mechanisms behind the suppression of transport anomalies.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
